docs.title: QooQuote
docs.text: This is the main page of the documentation. Here you can find all the information you need to get started with the project.

docs.title: What is QooQuote?
docs.text: QooQuote is a project that a social app that allows users share any Quote they like with their friends. It is a simple app that allows users to share their favorite quotes from a movie or a book or a song with their friends. The app is designed to be simple and easy to use. It is a great way to share your favorite quotes with your friends. You can also follow your friends and see what quotes they have shared, and you can like and comment on their quotes.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Full design can be found in the on Figma. But you may also find the basics in the following sections.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
This document provides a guide for developers who want to contribute to the QooQuote project. It covers the following topics:

  • Setting up the development environment
  • Running the project
  • Testing
  • Deployment
  • Contributing

docs.title: System Architecture
docs.text: This project is still in development and may change in the future. Be sure to check the latest version of the document.

docs.title: Creating a new user
docs.text:
Users can create a new account by providing their email and password.

sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User
    participant Frontend as Frontend
    participant Backend as Backend
    participant DB as Database

    User->>Frontend: Enter email and password
    Frontend->>Backend: Send sign-up request
    Backend->>Backend: Validate input
    Backend->>Backend: Hash password
    Backend->>DB: Save user data (email, hashed password)
    DB-->>Backend: Confirmation
    Backend-->>Frontend: Sign-up successful
    Frontend-->>User: Account created

Users can also sign up using their Spotify or Pinterest account.

sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User
    participant Frontend as Frontend
    participant Backend as Backend
    participant OAuth as Spotify/Pinterest
    participant DB as Database

    User->>Frontend: Select Spotify/Pinterest sign-up
    Frontend->>Backend: Send sign-up request
    Backend->>OAuth: Request user authentication
    OAuth-->>Backend: Return user data (ID, email, etc.)
    Backend->>DB: Save user data (OAuth provider details)
    DB-->>Backend: Confirmation
    Backend-->>Frontend: Sign-up successful
    Frontend-->>User: Account created

docs.title: Logging in
docs.text:
At this point, we do not plan to use an external auth provider like Auth0, but we might consider it in the future. Either way, the architecture would remain the same, with the only difference being the external provider handling the authentication process.

sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User
    participant Frontend as Frontend
    participant Backend as Backend
    participant DB as Database
    participant JWT as JWT Service

    User->>Frontend: Enter email and password
    Frontend->>Backend: Send login request
    Backend->>DB: Validate user credentials
    DB-->>Backend: Return user data (if valid)
    Backend->>JWT: Generate JWT (Access + Refresh tokens)
    JWT-->>Backend: Return tokens
    Backend-->>Frontend: Login successful (Send tokens)
    Frontend-->>User: Logged in

    Note over Frontend, User: Frontend stores tokens securely (e.g., HTTP-only cookies)

Nowadays, the most common way to handle authentication is by using JWT (JSON Web Tokens). This is a secure way to authenticate users and keep them logged in. The frontend stores the JWT tokens securely (e.g., in HTTP-only cookies) and sends them with every request to the backend. The backend then verifies the tokens and allows access to the requested resources if the tokens are valid. It is common to make users log in again after a certain period of inactivity, or when the access token expires. In this case, the frontend can use the refresh token to get a new access token without the user having to log in again.

sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User
    participant Frontend as Frontend
    participant Backend as Backend
    participant JWT as JWT Service

    User->>Frontend: Make an API request
    Frontend->>Backend: Send request with Access Token
    Backend->>JWT: Validate Access Token
    alt Token is valid
        JWT-->>Backend: Token valid
        Backend-->>Frontend: Process API request
        Frontend-->>User: Return response
    else Token is invalid or expired
        JWT-->>Backend: Token invalid
        Backend->>Frontend: Request token refresh
        Frontend->>Backend: Send Refresh Token
        Backend->>JWT: Validate and issue new tokens
        JWT-->>Backend: Return new Access and Refresh tokens
        Backend-->>Frontend: New tokens issued
        Frontend->>Backend: Retry API request with new Access Token
        Backend-->>Frontend: Process request and return response
        Frontend-->>User: Return response
    end

docs.title: Creating a new post
docs.text:
Users can create new posts. They can add a title, description, and tags to their post. They can also add images and videos to their post.

sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User
    participant Frontend as Frontend
    participant Backend as Backend
    participant DB as Database

    User->>Frontend: Create post request (tag, post_text, media, public, etc.)
    Frontend->>Backend: Send post payload
    Backend->>Backend: Validate payload (e.g., required fields)
    alt Payload valid
        Backend->>DB: Store post data
        DB-->>Backend: Confirm post stored
        Backend-->>Frontend: Return success response (post ID, etc.)
        Frontend-->>User: Post created successfully
    else Payload invalid
        Backend-->>Frontend: Return error response (validation failed)
        Frontend-->>User: Display error message
    end

docs.title: Reposting
docs.text:
Users can repost content they find interesting, similar to retweeting.

sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User
    participant Frontend as Frontend
    participant Backend as Backend
    participant DB as Database

    User->>Frontend: Click repost on a post
    Frontend->>Backend: Send repost request (original post ID)
    Backend->>DB: Create repost entry
    DB-->>Backend: Confirm repost stored
    Backend->>DB: Update original post metrics
    DB-->>Backend: Confirm metrics updated
    Backend-->>Frontend: Return success response
    Frontend-->>User: Repost successful

docs.title: Deleting a post
docs.text:
Users can delete their own posts.

sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User
    participant Frontend as Frontend
    participant Backend as Backend
    participant DB as Database

    User->>Frontend: Request post deletion
    Frontend->>Backend: Send delete request
    Backend->>Backend: Verify user ownership
    alt User owns post
        Backend->>DB: Mark post as deleted
        DB-->>Backend: Confirm deletion
        Backend-->>Frontend: Return success response
        Frontend-->>User: Post deleted successfully
    else User doesn't own post
        Backend-->>Frontend: Return error response
        Frontend-->>User: Permission denied
    end

docs.title: Archiving a post
docs.text:
Users can archive posts to hide them from their public profile while keeping them for personal reference.

sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User
    participant Frontend as Frontend
    participant Backend as Backend
    participant DB as Database

    User->>Frontend: Request post archive
    Frontend->>Backend: Send archive request
    Backend->>Backend: Verify user ownership
    alt User owns post
        Backend->>DB: Update post status to archived
        DB-->>Backend: Confirm archive status
        Backend-->>Frontend: Return success response
        Frontend-->>User: Post archived successfully
    else User doesn't own post
        Backend-->>Frontend: Return error response
        Frontend-->>User: Permission denied
    end

docs.title: Feed
docs.text:
The core of an app like QooQuote is the feed. This is where users can see posts from other users. The feed can be filtered by tags, and users can like and comment on posts. The feed is a complex system that requires a lot of work to get right. We are planning to use a cumulative filtering system to show the posts to the user. This means that we will filter the posts by tags, and then by the user's preferences. This way, the user will see the most relevant posts first.

sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User
    participant Frontend as Frontend
    participant Backend as Backend
    participant RecoSystem as Recommendation System
    participant DB as Database

    User->>Frontend: Request recommendations
    Frontend->>Backend: Forward recommendation request
    Backend->>RecoSystem: Fetch personalized recommendations
    RecoSystem->>DB: Query user data and preferences
    DB-->>RecoSystem: Return user data and preferences
    RecoSystem->>DB: Query recommendation data (e.g., posts, tags, media)
    DB-->>RecoSystem: Return relevant data
    RecoSystem-->>Backend: Return generated recommendations
    Backend-->>Frontend: Send recommendations to frontend
    Frontend-->>User: Display recommendations

docs.title: The Algorithm
docs.text:
The recommendation system is the core of the feed. It is responsible for generating personalized recommendations for each user. The system uses a combination of collaborative filtering and content-based filtering to generate recommendations. Inspired by Twitter's recommendation system, we will use a hybrid approach to generate recommendations.

Since this system is too complex for a short period of time, we will start with a simple recommendation system that will generate recommendations based on the user's preferences. We will use a simple collaborative filtering algorithm to generate recommendations using pg-vector.

graph TD
    A[User Request] --> B[Backend Recommendation Endpoint]
    B --> C[Fetch User Preferences]
    B --> D[Fetch User Embedding via pg-vector]
    C --> E[Apply Cumulative Filtering]
    D --> F[Apply Content-Based Filtering]
    E --> G[Combine & Rank Hybrid Approach]
    F --> G
    G --> H[Final Recommendations]
and the pg-vector flow looks like this 
sequenceDiagram
    participant CL as Client (e.g., psql or app)
    participant PG as PostgreSQL
    participant PV as pg-vector Extension

    CL->>PG: SELECT * FROM posts<br>ORDER BY embedding <-> query_embedding<br>LIMIT 5
    PG->>PV: Calculate vector similarity
    PV-->>PG: Return sorted results
    PG-->>CL: Top matching rows

docs.title: Database
docs.text:
We will use PSQL for the database.

This project is still in development and may change in the future. Be sure to check the latest version of the document.

docs.title: Schema
docs.text:
erDiagram
    %% ------------------
    %%      USERS
    %% ------------------
    USERS {
        int       id PK
        string    email
        string    passwordHash
        string    displayName
        datetime  createdAt
        datetime  updatedAt
    }

    %% ------------------
    %%      POSTS
    %% ------------------
    POSTS {
        int       id PK
        int       userId FK "references USERS(id)"
        int       genreId FK "references GENRES(id)" 
        string    quoteText
        string    mediaUrl
        boolean   isPublic
        boolean   isArchived
        datetime  createdAt
        datetime  updatedAt
    }

    %% ------------------
    %%      GENRES
    %% ------------------
    GENRES {
        int       id PK
        string    genreName
        string    description
    }

    %% ------------------
    %%      TAGS
    %% ------------------
    TAGS {
        int       id PK
        string    tagName
    }

    %% ------------------
    %%      POST_TAGS
    %% ------------------
    POST_TAGS {
        int       postId FK "references POSTS(id)"
        int       tagId  FK "references TAGS(id)"
        %% optionally add a composite PK of (postId, tagId) 
        datetime  createdAt
    }

    %% ------------------
    %%      COMMENTS
    %% ------------------
    COMMENTS {
        int       id PK
        int       userId FK "references USERS(id)"
        int       postId FK "references POSTS(id)"
        string    commentText
        datetime  createdAt
        datetime  updatedAt
    }

    %% ------------------
    %%      LIKES
    %% ------------------
    LIKES {
        int       id PK
        int       userId FK "references USERS(id)"
        int       postId FK "references POSTS(id)"
        datetime  createdAt
    }

    %% ------------------
    %%      REPOSTS
    %% ------------------
    REPOSTS {
        int       id PK
        int       userId FK "references USERS(id)"
        int       originalPostId FK "references POSTS(id)"
        datetime  createdAt
    }

    %% ------------------
    %%      FOLLOWS
    %% ------------------
    FOLLOWS {
        int       followerId FK "references USERS(id)"
        int       followedId FK "references USERS(id)"
        datetime  createdAt
        %% Add a composite PK if desired (followerId, followedId)
    }

    %% ------------------
    %%  USER_PREFERENCES
    %% ------------------
    USER_PREFERENCES {
        int       id PK
        int       userId FK "references USERS(id)"
        string    preferenceKey
        string    preferenceValue
        datetime  createdAt
        datetime  updatedAt
    }

    %% ------------------
    %%    FRIENDSHIPS
    %% ------------------
    FRIENDSHIPS {
        int       id PK
        int       requestorUserId FK "references USERS(id)"
        int       acceptorUserId FK "references USERS(id)"
        string    status
        datetime  createdAt
        datetime  updatedAt
    }

    %% ------------------
    %%    RELATIONSHIPS
    %% ------------------

    %% 1 user can create many posts
    USERS ||--o{ POSTS : "creates"

    %% 1 user can have many comments; 1 comment belongs to 1 user
    USERS ||--o{ COMMENTS : "writes"

    %% 1 user can have many likes
    USERS ||--o{ LIKES : "likes"

    %% 1 user can make many reposts
    USERS ||--o{ REPOSTS : "makes"

    %% 1 post can have many comments; 1 comment belongs to 1 post
    POSTS ||--o{ COMMENTS : "has"

    %% 1 post can have many likes
    POSTS ||--o{ LIKES : "has"

    %% 1 post can be reposted many times
    POSTS ||--o{ REPOSTS : "is reposted"

    %% users follow many users (one-way)
    USERS ||--o{ FOLLOWS : "follows"

    %% 1 user can have many preferences
    USERS ||--o{ USER_PREFERENCES : "has preferences"

    %% FRIENDSHIPS: each row tracks requestor vs. acceptor
    USERS ||--o{ FRIENDSHIPS : "requests friendship"
    USERS ||--o{ FRIENDSHIPS : "accepts friendship"

    %% 1 GENRE can have many POSTS
    GENRES ||--o{ POSTS : "categorizes"

    %% many-to-many: POSTS & TAGS join through POST_TAGS
    POSTS }o--o{ POST_TAGS : "tagged by"
    TAGS }o--o{ POST_TAGS : "tags many"

docs.title: GraphQL Schema
docs.text:
This project is still in development and may change in the future. Be sure to check the latest version of the document.

########################################
# TYPES
########################################

type User {
  id: ID!
  email: String!
  displayName: String
  createdAt: String
  updatedAt: String
}

type Post {
  id: ID!
  userId: ID!
  genreId: ID
  quoteText: String!
  mediaUrl: String
  isPublic: Boolean
  isArchived: Boolean
  createdAt: String
  updatedAt: String
  user: User         
  genre: Genre       
  tags: [Tag]       
  } 

type Genre {
  id: ID!
  genreName: String!
  description: String
}

type Tag {
  id: ID!
  tagName: String!
}

type Comment {
  id: ID!
  userId: ID!
  postId: ID!
  commentText: String!
  createdAt: String
  updatedAt: String
  user: User
  post: Post
}

type Like {
  id: ID!
  userId: ID!
  postId: ID!
  createdAt: String
}

type Follow {
  followerId: ID!
  followedId: ID!
  createdAt: String
}

type Repost {
  id: ID!
  userId: ID!
  originalPostId: ID!
  createdAt: String
}

type Friendship {
  id: ID!
  requestorUserId: ID!
  acceptorUserId: ID!
  status: String
  createdAt: String
  updatedAt: String
}

type UserPreferences {
  id: ID!
  userId: ID!
  preferenceKey: String!
  preferenceValue: String!
  createdAt: String
  updatedAt: String
}

########################################
# QUERIES
########################################

type Query {
  # Users
  getUser(id: ID!): User
  getUsers: [User]

  # Posts
  getPost(id: ID!): Post
  getPosts: [Post]

  # Genres
  getGenre(id: ID!): Genre
  getGenres: [Genre]

  # Tags
  getTag(id: ID!): Tag
  getTags: [Tag]

  # Comments
  getCommentsForPost(postId: ID!): [Comment]

  # Follows
  getFollows(userId: ID!): [Follow]

  # Friendships
  getFriendships(userId: ID!): [Friendship]

  # Preferences
  getUserPreferences(userId: ID!): [UserPreferences]
}

########################################
# MUTATIONS
########################################

type Mutation {
  # User-related
  createUser(email: String!, password: String!, displayName: String): User
  loginUser(email: String!, password: String!): String 

  # Posts
  createPost(userId: ID!, genreId: ID, quoteText: String!, mediaUrl: String, isPublic: Boolean): Post
  archivePost(postId: ID!): Post
  deletePost(postId: ID!): Boolean

  # Comments
  createComment(userId: ID!, postId: ID!, commentText: String!): Comment
  deleteComment(commentId: ID!): Boolean

  # Likes
  likePost(userId: ID!, postId: ID!): Like
  unlikePost(likeId: ID!): Boolean

  # Reposts
  repost(userId: ID!, originalPostId: ID!): Repost

  # Follows
  followUser(followerId: ID!, followedId: ID!): Follow
  unfollowUser(followerId: ID!, followedId: ID!): Boolean

  # Friendships
  sendFriendRequest(requestorUserId: ID!, acceptorUserId: ID!): Friendship
  respondFriendRequest(friendshipId: ID!, status: String!): Friendship

  # Preferences
  setUserPreference(userId: ID!, preferenceKey: String!, preferenceValue: String!): UserPreferences
}

docs.title: UML
docs.text: This project is still in development and may change in the future. Be sure to check the latest version of the document.

docs.title: Users
docs.text:
classDiagram
    class User {
        +String email
        +String password
        +String username
        +String id
        +createAccount()
        +login()
    }

    class Account {
        +String userId
        +AuthType authType
        +DateTime createdAt
        +Boolean isVerified
        +verifyEmail()
        +updateProfile()
    }

    class OAuthProvider {
        +String providerName
        +String clientId
        +String clientSecret
        +authenticate()
        +getAccessToken()
        +getUserInfo()
    }

    class PinterestAuth {
        +authenticate()
        +getAccessToken()
        +getUserInfo()
    }

    class SpotifyAuth {
        +authenticate()
        +getAccessToken()
        +getUserInfo()
    }

    class EmailAuth {
        +String email
        +String hashedPassword
        +validatePassword()
        +hashPassword()
        +sendVerificationEmail()
    }

    OAuthProvider <|-- PinterestAuth
    OAuthProvider <|-- SpotifyAuth
    User "1" -- "1" Account
    Account -- EmailAuth
    Account -- OAuthProvider

docs.title: Posts
docs.text:
classDiagram
    class Post {
        -String id
        -String userId
        -String title
        -String description
        -List<Tag> tags
        -List<Media> media
        -boolean isPublic
        -PostStatus status
        -DateTime createdAt
        -DateTime updatedAt
        +create()
        +delete()
        +archive()
        +repost()
        +validatePost()
    }

    class PostService {
        +createPost(PostDTO)
        +deletePost(String postId, String userId)
        +archivePost(String postId, String userId)
        +repostPost(String postId, String userId)
        -validateOwnership(String postId, String userId)
    }

    class PostRepository {
        <<interface>>
        +save(Post)
        +findById(String)
        +delete(String)
        +updateStatus(String, PostStatus)
        +createRepost(String, String)
    }

    class Media {
        -String id
        -String url
        -MediaType type
        -String postId
    }

    class Tag {
        -String id
        -String name
    }

    class PostStatus {
        <<enumeration>>
        ACTIVE
        DELETED
        ARCHIVED
        REPOSTED
    }

    class MediaType {
        <<enumeration>>
        IMAGE
        VIDEO
    }

    Post --> PostStatus
    Post "1" *-- "*" Media
    Post "1" *-- "*" Tag
    Post --> PostService
    PostService --> PostRepository
    Media --> MediaType

docs.title: Feed
docs.text:
classDiagram
    class Feed {
        -String userId
        -List<Post> posts
        -FeedPreferences preferences
        +getFeed(page, size)
        +refreshFeed()
        +filterByTags(tags)
    }

    class RecommendationService {
        -vectorStore: PgVector
        +getRecommendations(userId)
        -applyCollaborativeFiltering()
        -applyContentBasedFiltering()
        -combineResults()
        -rankPosts()
    }

    class PgVector {
        -Connection connection
        +search(embedding, limit)
        +insert(document, embedding)
        +delete(id)
        +update(id, embedding)
    }

    class FeedPreferences {
        -String userId
        -List<String> preferredTags
        -List<String> blockedUsers
        -List<String> followedUsers
        -EmbeddingVector userEmbedding
        +updatePreferences()
    }

    class Post {
        -String id
        -String content
        -List<String> tags
        -EmbeddingVector embedding
        -DateTime createdAt
        +generateEmbedding()
    }

    class EmbeddingVector {
        -float[] vector
        -int dimensions
        +similarity(EmbeddingVector)
        +normalize()
    }

    Feed --> RecommendationService
    RecommendationService --> PgVector
    Feed --> FeedPreferences
    RecommendationService --> Post
    Post --> EmbeddingVector
    FeedPreferences --> EmbeddingVector
SQL may look like this: 
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;

CREATE TABLE feed_preferences (
    user_id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    preferred_tags TEXT[],
    blocked_users UUID[],
    followed_users UUID[],
    user_embedding vector(384)
);

CREATE TABLE post_embeddings (
    post_id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    embedding vector(384),
    FOREIGN KEY (post_id) REFERENCES posts(id)
);

CREATE INDEX post_embedding_idx ON post_embeddings 
USING ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops)
WITH (lists = 100);

docs.title: Authentication Flow
docs.text:
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> CheckAuthStatus
    CheckAuthStatus --> ShowLoginForm: Not Authenticated
    CheckAuthStatus --> ShowMainFeed: Authenticated

    ShowLoginForm --> ValidateCredentials: Submit
    ValidateCredentials --> GenerateTokens: Valid
    ValidateCredentials --> ShowError: Invalid
    ShowError --> ShowLoginForm

    GenerateTokens --> StoreTokens
    StoreTokens --> ShowMainFeed
    ShowMainFeed --> [*]

docs.title: Post Creation Flow
docs.text:
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> InitiatePostCreation
    InitiatePostCreation --> ShowPostForm

    ShowPostForm --> ValidateContent: Submit
    ValidateContent --> ProcessMedia: Valid
    ValidateContent --> ShowValidationError: Invalid
    ShowValidationError --> ShowPostForm

    ProcessMedia --> GenerateEmbeddings
    GenerateEmbeddings --> SavePost
    SavePost --> UpdateFeed
    UpdateFeed --> [*]

docs.title: Feed Generation Flow
docs.text:
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> ValidateRequest

    ValidateRequest --> CheckCache: Valid Request
    ValidateRequest --> ReturnError: Invalid Request

    CheckCache --> ReturnCachedFeed: Cache Hit
    CheckCache --> InitiateFeedGeneration: Cache Miss

    state FeedGeneration {
        [*] --> LoadUserContext

        state LoadUserContext {
            [*] --> FetchUserPreferences
            FetchUserPreferences --> FetchUserHistory
            FetchUserHistory --> BuildUserProfile
        }

        LoadUserContext --> QueryContent

        state QueryContent {
            [*] --> FetchRelevantPosts
            FetchRelevantPosts --> ApplyContentFilters
            ApplyContentFilters --> CalculateSimilarityScores
        }

        QueryContent --> RankContent

        state RankContent {
            [*] --> ApplyRecommendationModel
            ApplyRecommendationModel --> SortByRelevance
            SortByRelevance --> ApplyDiversityRules
        }
    }

    InitiateFeedGeneration --> FeedGeneration
    FeedGeneration --> OptimizeFeed

    OptimizeFeed --> PaginateResults
    PaginateResults --> CacheFeed
    CacheFeed --> ReturnFeed

    ReturnFeed --> [*]
    ReturnError --> [*]
    ReturnCachedFeed --> [*]

    state ErrorHandling {
        TimeoutError --> ReturnError
        ValidationError --> ReturnError
        DataError --> ReturnError
    }

docs.title: User Interaction Flow
docs.text:
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> ViewPost

    ViewPost --> ProcessInteraction

    state InteractionTypes <<choice>>
    ProcessInteraction --> InteractionTypes

    InteractionTypes --> Like: Like
    InteractionTypes --> Comment: Comment
    InteractionTypes --> Share: Share
    InteractionTypes --> Save: Save

    Like --> UpdateMetrics
    Comment --> UpdateMetrics
    Share --> UpdateMetrics
    Save --> UpdateMetrics

    UpdateMetrics --> RecalculateRecommendations
    RecalculateRecommendations --> [*]
